message:
feat(pager,btree,obs): page-cache efficiency snapshots and cursor position stamps

Adds the low-level primitives that the higher-level query pipeline work
in this batch depends on. All additions are purely additive and the new
symbols are re-exported at their crate roots so downstream crates can
opt in without touching any pager/btree internals.

fsqlite-types
-------------
* `PageData::from_shared(Arc<[u8]>)` constructor for building page
  snapshots out of already-shared immutable buffers without forcing a
  copy through `PageDataRepr::Owned`. The pager uses this on read paths
  where it already holds an `Arc<[u8]>` page image.

fsqlite-observability
---------------------
* New `page_cache` submodule defining `PageCacheEfficiencySnapshot`, the
  shared wire-type used by pagers to report hit/miss/admission counters
  plus derived efficiency ratios to the rest of the system. Re-exported
  at the crate root so consumers can depend on it through the
  observability facade rather than reaching into the pager.

fsqlite-pager
-------------
* Takes an explicit workspace dependency on fsqlite-observability so it
  can return `PageCacheEfficiencySnapshot` from the new
  `PageCache::efficiency_snapshot` / `SimplePager::cache_efficiency_snapshot`
  entry points.
* `page_cache.rs` tracks the counters needed to populate the snapshot
  and exposes `efficiency_snapshot(self)` to hand them off without
  holding the cache lock during serialization.
* `pager.rs` grows `cache_efficiency_snapshot()` which walks the active
  backend and returns the unified snapshot. The new read paths use
  `PageData::from_shared` to avoid an extra copy when the pager already
  owns an `Arc<[u8]>` page image.

fsqlite-btree
-------------
* Introduces `CursorPositionStamp` — an opaque, comparable stamp that
  records where a cursor currently sits (root page + path + slot) so
  that higher layers can detect whether a cursor has moved between two
  points in time without having to re-materialize a key. `BtCursor`
  exposes `position_stamp()` and `balance.rs` keeps stamps coherent
  across page splits/merges and cursor re-anchoring after balance. The
  stamp is re-exported from `fsqlite_btree::cursor` and the crate root.

No behavioural changes on their own — these primitives light up in the
companion commit that wires them through the parser, planner, VDBE and
core connection state.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
